sections.paragraph:
fut. יִקֹּר to bore, to pierce, especially used of boring out any one’s eye, 1Sa 11:2 Pro 30:17, “the ravens of the valley shall pick it (the eye) out;” Num 16:14 Jud. 16:21 [Both Piel].-(Arab. id.; Eth. ነቋረ፡ to be blind of one eye, ነቀለ፡ to pull out.)

Piel id. Job 30:17, לַיְלָה עֲצָמִי נִקַּר “the night pierces my bones,” i.e. by night my bones are, as it were, pierced with pain; compare Job 3:3.

Pual, to be dug out, Isa 51:1, “the quarry (whence) נֻקַּרְתֶּם ye were digged;” metaph. used of the ancestors of a nation. Hence-
